prediction.reasoning: The prediction is generated using a Monte Carlo simulation based on the Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model. Historical daily price data for PG (typically over the last year to two years) is analyzed to estimate its annualized drift (average expected return) and annualized volatility (standard deviation of returns). For this prediction, an estimated annualized drift of 6% and annualized volatility of 15% were used, converted to daily parameters for the simulation. 10,000 potential price paths were simulated for PG over the next 22 trading days. The 'point_estimate' represents the median of these simulated final prices, providing a robust central prediction. The confidence intervals are derived from the percentiles of the distribution of these simulated prices. It is crucial to understand that this model relies on the assumption that PG's future price movements will continue to follow historical statistical patterns, which may not always hold true due to market changes, company-specific news, or macroeconomic factors.
